name: refac-01-monolith-to-modules
initialPrompt: Our Rails monolith has grown to 400k lines and deploy times are hitting 45 minutes. I want to split it into independently deployable modules: start with the billing subsystem, then the notifications subsystem, and finally shared auth middleware that both can use. Each module should have its own database schema, its own CI pipeline, and clear API contracts with the rest of the monolith so we can strangle it over time.

blocks.text: Extract billing and notifications into independently deployable modules with clear API contracts
successCriteria: Monolith deploy time drops below 20 min
Zero cross-module DB joins in production

blocks.title: Extract billing module with own DB schema

blocks.question: Inter-module communication: synchronous REST or async events?
blocks.rationale: Decouples modules temporally; billing events trigger notifications naturally.

blocks.description: Splitting shared tables causes data migration failures in production
blocks.mitigation: Blue-green schema migration with read-replica validation before cutover

blocks.question: Should shared auth middleware be a separate deployed service or a shared gem/package?

blocks.text: Extract billing, notifications, and shared auth into independently deployable modules with explicit API contracts
successCriteria: Monolith deploy time drops below 20 min
Zero cross-module DB joins in production
Shared auth library published as internal package

blocks.title: Spike: validate strangler-fig routing layer with production traffic sample
acceptance: Routing layer handles 1k req/s without latency regression

blocks.title: Extract billing module with own DB schema
Extract shared auth as internal gem published to private registry
acceptance: Both billing and notifications modules use it

blocks.question: Inter-module communication: synchronous REST or async events?
blocks.rationale: Decouples modules temporally; billing events trigger notifications naturally.

blocks.description: Splitting shared tables causes data migration failures in production
blocks.mitigation: Blue-green schema migration with read-replica validation before cutover

blocks.question: Should shared auth middleware be a separate deployed service or a shared gem/package?

idChangedButCorresponding.note: Same decision (REST vs async events) but the agent renamed the id from dec-comms-pattern to dec-comms-channel during revise. The intent is unchanged; the AC-13 re-anchoring fallback must map a comment on dec-comms-pattern to dec-comms-channel.

decoy.note: Genuinely new block. Shares surface tokens 'extract' and 'module' with task-notifications-extract, making it a trap. A comment on task-notifications-extract must re-attach to task-notifications-extract, NOT to task-auth-package.

notes: Renumbering pressure: a spike task is inserted near top shifting task-audit-deps down by one position; a new task-auth-package block is added; dec-comms-pattern is renamed to dec-comms-channel; oq-shared-auth is renamed to oq-auth-deploy. Positional scheme loses alignment after the spike insertion. expectedPreservedIds excludes dec-comms-pattern (id changed) and oq-shared-auth (id changed) and the two new tasks.
